enonce.texte: Ce questionnaire évalue votre maîtrise du vocabulaire essentiel lié à la violence et aux conflits. Lisez chaque phrase et choisissez la bonne réponse.

questions.texte: Match the word 'abuse' with its correct definition.

sousQuestions.texte: B) Cruel or violent treatment of someone

questions.texte: Which word best completes the sentence? 'After the war, the country entered a long period of ______.'
Choose the word that means 'a person who tries to help two groups reach an agreement'.

intitule: Applying Conflict Resolution in Real Life

enonce.texte:
Lisez la situation ci-dessous, puis répondez aux questions en utilisant le vocabulaire et les stratégies de résolution de conflits.

Situation : Dans votre quartier à Douala, deux groupes de jeunes se disputent un terrain de football. Des insultes ont été échangées et une bagarre a éclaté hier. Un garçon a été blessé. Vous êtes le délégué de votre classe et vous voulez aider.

questions.texte: Classify the following actions as either 'escalating violence' or 'promoting conflict resolution'.

sousQuestions.texte: Organiser une réunion avec les leaders des deux groupes.
Chercher des bâtons pour se défendre.
Informer un adulte de confiance (un enseignant ou un chef de quartier).
Publier des menaces sur les réseaux sociaux contre l'autre groupe.

questions.texte: Propose a step-by-step negotiation plan to solve the conflict over the football field. Use at least three vocabulary words from the lesson (conflict, mediation, negotiation, peace, abuse, fight).
questions.reponse: Un plan acceptable pourrait inclure : 1) Cesser les insultes (stop verbal abuse). 2) Organiser une médiation avec un adulte. 3) Établir un planning pour l'utilisation du terrain (negotiation). 4) Signer un accord de paix.

questions.texte: Justify why using physical violence (fighting) is never a good solution to a conflict. Give two specific reasons based on the lesson.
questions.reponse: 1) Physical violence causes injuries and can lead to death. 2) Violence creates hatred and makes future mediation more difficult. 3) Violence can lead to legal consequences (arrest, prison).

intitule: Grammar in Context: Conditionals and Conflict

enonce.texte: Complétez ces phrases sur les conflits en utilisant le type conditionnel correct (Type 1, 2 ou 3) et le vocabulaire de la leçon.

questions.texte: If people ______ (learn) to negotiate, fewer fights would break out.
If the mediator ______ (arrive) earlier yesterday, the situation would not have escalated.
If you insult someone, you ______ (create) more conflict.
Rewrite this sentence using 'unless': 'If you do not stop the abuse, the conflict will continue.'
questions.reponse: Unless you stop the abuse, the conflict will continue.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: A Conflict in the Sahel

enonce.texte:
Lisez le texte et répondez aux questions.

In the Far North Region of Cameroon, conflicts between farmers and herders have increased. Due to climate change, water and land are scarce. Herders move their cattle onto farmland, destroying crops. Farmers sometimes attack the herders. This cycle of violence has caused many deaths. Local chiefs and NGOs are now organizing peace committees and mediation sessions to resolve the disputes.

questions.texte: Identify the main cause of the conflict mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Climate change leading to scarcity of water and land.

questions.texte: Evaluate the effectiveness of using peace committees in this context. Give one advantage and one possible limitation.
questions.reponse: Advantage: They bring local leaders together and promote dialogue. Limitation: They may lack legal power to enforce agreements.

questions.texte: Create a slogan (in English) that could be used by an NGO to promote conflict resolution between farmers and herders.
questions.reponse: Example: 'Land for all, peace for all' or 'Negotiate, don't escalate'.
